event: maintained on oral pancreatic enzyme therapy for exocrine pancreatic insufficiency
presented to a medical center in India with pain
underwent CT scan of the abdomen
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics (first admission)
admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis (second admission)
CT scan performed in the emergency department
fatty atrophy of both the pancreatic head and uncinate process
the pancreatic duct was filled with large calcifications with a possible obstruction
on the second day of admission developed fever (102°F)
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
markedly dilated pancreatic duct (1.9 cm) with an 8.7 × 7.6 mm calculus
common bile duct was prominent with no obvious obstructing calculi
major papilla spontaneously expelling frank pus
evacuation of more than 5 ml of yellow pus
marked dilatation of the main pancreatic duct with a single distal calculus
guide wire introduced into the pancreatic duct
acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct (ASPD)
reevaluation of the pancreas with a contrast enhanced CT scan (next day)
inflammatory changes in the fat surrounding the body and tail of the pancreas
no evidence of pancreatic necrosis or fluid collection
the calculus showing distal migration towards the sphincter of Oddi
transferred from the intensive care unit
follow-up at 1 month after discharge revealed no further complications
follow-up at 3 months after discharge revealed no further complications
